steps.title: Find the clauses
steps.content: The first complete idea is "these consonants are rare." The next complete idea is that in some dialects of Mandarin, four such consonants are relatively common. The word "however" marks a contrast between the rarity in many languages and the relative commonness in those Mandarin dialects.

steps.title: Join the contrast correctly
steps.content: A comma alone would create a comma splice because it would join two independent clauses without adequate punctuation. No punctuation would create a run-on, and "rare and in some dialects" would not cleanly coordinate the two complete ideas. A semicolon correctly links the two closely related independent clauses before the contrast word "however." The answer is C.
